summary: Close-out pass on coder v9. Coder pivoted A1 from rename + [Obsolete] to full deletion of the channel-migration surface (Ingi: 'I actually feel like all this Migration on channel is not needed now'). Verified deletion is complete (no dangling refs in src or tests), A3 fix is correct (using StreamReader + ResolveEncoding + leaveOpen, regression test passes), and tests match (2755/2755 C#, 203 + 6 deliberate fixture fails PLang). PASS.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: agree — B1 + L1 fixes still in tree; coder v9 didn't touch Events.
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: agree — Stage9 test deletions are correct (testing code that no longer exists); 1 new encoding regression added.
previous_reviews_assessed.security: agree — pre-merge concerns over-resolved by deletion.

v1_findings_status.A2: mooted by deletion; intent carries forward to Stage 9 transport

findings.issue: Mentions 'Channels that migrate across devices' as a feature; with the surface deleted this should read clearly as forward-looking/roadmap, not current behaviour.
findings.impact: Reader could be misled that migration exists today.
findings.suggestion: Label as future/roadmap or move to a roadmap doc. For docs bot.

findings.issue: Architectural fit: deletion was the right call vs rename + [Obsolete]. When cross-device transport returns under Stage 9, design the envelope from scratch with security review — don't resurrect this surface.
findings.impact: None on this branch; carry-forward note.
findings.suggestion: Note for the future Stage 9 architect.

findings.issue: A2 (permission gate on Variables-snapshot exposure) carries forward to the Stage 9 transport rebuild. Must be designed in from the start, not bolted on.
findings.suggestion: Note for the future Stage 9 architect.
